summary: BUG FIX VERIFIED: Page reload issue after delete in Transfers Queue and Card Requests is FIXED. Comprehensive testing showed NO random reloads after 15+ seconds monitoring. The toastRef + useMemo fix successfully prevents the toast object recreation cascade that was causing fetchTransfers to be recreated and triggering unwanted useEffect calls.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is correctly implemented - toastRef pattern prevents fetchTransfers dependency on toast object
useMemo in Toast.js ensures stable toast object reference across renders
Both AdminTransfersQueue.js and AdminCardRequestsQueue.js use the same toastRef pattern for consistency
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix for page reload after delete is verified and working. The root cause was toast object recreation causing fetchTransfers to be recreated via useCallback deps, triggering useEffect. Fix applied: toastRef in components + useMemo in Toast.js.

bug_fix_verification.bug_description: Page unexpectedly reloads/refreshes 2-4 seconds after admin deletes a transfer from Transfers Queue
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: Toast object from useToast() was recreated on every ToastProvider render. This caused fetchTransfers useCallback (which had toast in deps) to be recreated, triggering the useEffect that calls fetchTransfers. Chain: delete -> toast.success/error -> toast recreated -> fetchTransfers recreated -> useEffect fires
fix_applied: AdminTransfersQueue.js: Added toastRef to keep stable toast reference, removed toast from fetchTransfers deps
Toast.js: Memoized toast object with useMemo to prevent consumer re-renders

tests_performed.details: URL stable, tab preserved, section param preserved, no page title disappearance
No reload after 10 seconds on second delete
Delete succeeded, URL stable, no reload
search=wowza preserved for 10+ seconds, input value retained
Closing detail panel doesn't trigger reload
